page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfc7.2xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs on an x86_64 architecture, utilizing an Intel Xeon 8369HB processor running at a clock speed of 3.3 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 16.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying on external storage solutions and a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the Intel Xeon processor delivers consistent mid-tier performance. The single-core stress-ng CPU performance score is 1578.48, while the multi-core score reaches 8323.63. Memory bandwidth tests show 465.63 GB/sec for cached small-block reads, dropping to 56.48 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. The instance demonstrates average capabilities in text processing, image processing, and software compilation. However, LLM inference benchmarks reveal a clear performance tradeoff, with prompt processing for a medium 7B model dropping to a poor rating of 11.86 tokens/sec, indicating limitations for larger AI workloads.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated CPU allocation and high clock speed make this instance a viable option for compute-heavy workloads that do not require massive memory footprints or local storage. The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio is optimized for tasks such as static web serving, where it achieves a throughput of 8.02 GB/sec, and database operations. It is also well-suited for lightweight serialization, compression tasks, and small-scale model inference, though it is not recommended for memory-intensive databases or large-scale deep learning.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfc7.2xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual server featuring 8 dedicated vCPUs and 8 physical cores on an x86_64 Intel Xeon 8369HB processor clocked at 3.3 GHz. Equipped with 16.0 GB of RAM (2.0 GB per core) and no local storage, this KVM-based instance provides a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps. Benchmark results place its CPU and memory performance in the average tier, with a single-core Geekbench score of 1319.0 and a multi-core score of 5101.0. While it handles static web serving at 8.02 GB/sec and Redis SET operations at 8.04 million operations per second, it exhibits weak performance on larger workloads like 7B LLM inference. This instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for dedicated, compute-bound workloads such as web serving, software compilation, and lightweight database operations that do not require local storage or high memory-to-CPU ratios.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfc7.2xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized server with 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 3 Gbps bandwidth, delivering balanced mid-tier performance for web serving and compilation.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfc7.2xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized server with 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 3 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A dedicated compute-optimized instance featuring high-frequency Intel Xeon processors and a balanced memory-to-core ratio for standard computational workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs running at 3.3 GHz
16.0 GB of system memory with 2.0 GB per core
3 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
Average performance in software compilation and web serving
Suitable for compute-bound tasks, static web hosting, and lightweight databases
